## Related Issue

<!--
Most PRs should reference an issue. Link it here.
For editorial/typo fixes, an issue is not required.

  Closes #
  Refs #
-->

---

## Type of Change

- [ ] Editorial (typo, grammar, formatting — no normative change)
- [ ] Clarification (normative language made more precise, no behavioral change)
- [ ] Spec bug fix (corrects an incorrect or unimplementable rule)
- [ ] Schema fix (corrects a JSON Schema without changing prose intent)
- [ ] Extension / new feature (adds new normative content)
- [ ] Breaking change (existing conforming archives or implementations would be affected)

---

## Affected Documents

- [ ] `specs/01-archive-structure.md`
- [ ] `specs/02-manifest-format.md`
- [ ] `specs/03-synchronization-protocol.md`
- [ ] `schemas/manifest.schema.json`
- [ ] `schemas/sync.schema.json`
- [ ] `README.md`
- [ ] Other (describe below)

---

## Description

<!-- Summarize what this PR changes and why. For normative changes, quote the before and after text. -->

---

## Checklist

- [ ] Normative requirements use RFC 2119 keywords in **ALL CAPS**.
- [ ] All JSON examples in modified sections are valid against the corresponding schema.
- [ ] If prose was changed, the JSON Schema is consistent with the updated prose (and vice versa).
- [ ] If a new field was added, it is documented in both the prose spec and the schema.
- [ ] Cross-references between spec documents are accurate and use relative Markdown links.
- [ ] The `"Version:"` header in spec documents has **not** been changed (unless this is an explicit version bump PR).
